key_insight: Zero-field phonon Hall viscosity provides a symmetry-sensitive fingerprint of altermagnetism, distinguishing it from ferromagnets and conventional antiferromagnets.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a magneto-acoustic route to detect broken symmetries and topological electronic structure in insulating altermagnets using strain-response measurements.
method: Derive the Hall viscosity tensor for altermagnets and identify the tensor components that separate magnetic phases.
Compute Hall viscosity microscopically in d-wave and g-wave altermagnet models.
Relate strong viscosity features to strain-space Berry curvature, gapped Dirac points, and Lifshitz transitions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical calculation results showing that specific Hall-viscosity tensor elements uniquely distinguish magnetic classes and vary strongly with spectrum features.
watch_item: Experimental interpretation may be challenging because the signal depends sensitively on electronic structure details and on clean magneto-acoustic measurement conditions.
